title: Claude Code Token Optimization — 3 Techniques
desc: 80-90% of tokens are spent on file exploration, not writing code. Three techniques to cut waste: (1) .claudeignore + lean CLAUDE.md + session management saves 30-40%. (2) Codebase pre-indexing saves 90%+ of exploration tokens. (3) 3-agent team (Architect/Builder/Reviewer) eliminates scope creep token waste.
steps: [CLAUDEIGNORE] Create .claudeignore in project root — lists files Claude must never read. Same concept as .gitignore. Exclude: node_modules/, .next/, dist/, bin/, obj/, .bak/, *.log, *.out. One-time setup, no maintenance needed.

[CLAUDE-MD] Keep CLAUDE.md under 200 lines. Every conversation loads it in full — bloated CLAUDE.md = constant token tax. Move detailed rules to separate topic files, reference them only when needed. Saves 5-10% per conversation.

[SESSION] Use /compact when conversation grows long (summarizes history, cuts context). Use /clear when switching to a completely new task. Check context% with wkappbot claude-usage — handoff at 8MB, urgent at 10MB.
[MCP] Only connect MCP servers you actively use. Each connected server adds tool definitions to every message. Disconnecting 3 unused servers gives immediate relief.

[PRE-INDEX] Tools like QMD pre-index the codebase so Claude jumps directly to the right file. Without indexing: glob → grep → read = 2700 tokens per lookup. With indexing: ~250 tokens. 91% reduction per lookup.
[PRE-INDEX-RULE] Add one line to CLAUDE.md: 'Always search the index before reading files.' This prevents Claude from blindly globbing the whole repo on every question.

[3-AGENT] Split work into 3 roles: (1) Architect — reads codebase, writes detailed brief (what files, what order, what changes). (2) Builder — implements ONLY what the brief specifies, nothing extra. (3) Reviewer — validates output, returns specific failures to Builder.

[3-AGENT-WHY] Without role separation, Claude reads the entire codebase for a login feature, adds untested CSS, writes unrequested tests. Every off-brief action = wasted tokens. Builder never needs to read files the Architect didn't specify.

[CLAUDE-MD-RULES] Add these 5 rules to CLAUDE.md: (1) Never re-read a file already in context. (2) No speculative tool calls. (3) Parallelize independent tool calls. (4) Route 20+ line non-essential output to sub-agents. (5) Never repeat what the user already explained.
[PRIORITY] Apply in order: first .claudeignore + lean CLAUDE.md (immediate, zero cost). Then pre-indexing (5 min setup, 90% exploration savings). Then 3-agent structure when project grows large.
[IMPACT] Combined: same token budget handles 2-3x more work. For Claude Pro users with tight limits, this effectively doubles monthly task capacity.
author: claude
